title: Owner Mission-Control dashboard: read-only observability layer over project-control (V1)
objective: Additive, read-only owner dashboard at /dashboard in apps/web that observes the existing project-control system WITHOUT weakening, duplicating, or mutating it. Derive all state deterministically from project-control JSON (tasks, master_plan, gates, blockers, checkpoints, state, config) plus one new canonical metadata file project-control/product-map.json (systems, explicit weights, owner-friendly titles/purposes, journey mapping) validated by project-control/product-map.schema.json + a stdlib validator + CI. Two deterministic auditable progress numbers (Engineering Completion, Launch Readiness) computed from ledger+weights, never AI-guessed. Views: Mission Control, Product Map (built-in SVG/CSS), Current Work, Roadmap, Activity, Blockers/Health, and Biggest-things-preventing-launch. Read-only public-repo GitHub status (main SHA, open/merged PRs, CI conclusions) with caching, freshness, and safe stale behavior. Internal-only (non-public runtime flag + INTERNAL banner); no secrets in frontend; no write paths; fail safe (show unavailable, never fabricate). Light theme reusing existing product tokens; zero new npm dependencies; thin-client (python+JSON locally, web build/tests in CI).
business_reason: The non-engineer owner needs to understand the entire project state visually in ~10 seconds (engineering vs launch-readiness progress, active/next/blocked/in-review work, CI health, which product systems are complete/partial/not-started, what changed, and what each technical task means in plain product language) without reading GitHub, task JSON, gate reports, or CI logs. Read-only observability over the authoritative project-control source of truth; commit count is explicitly rejected as a progress measure (owner directive 2026-07-22).
inputs: project-control/tasks/*.json (status, progress_percent, dependencies, required_gates, reconciliation.merged_pr, accepted_at, progress_log)
project-control/gates/*.json (gate_id, reviewer, role, result, reviewed_at) - per-task gate state = required_gates INTERSECT result:PASS
project-control/blockers/*.json (status:open + affects/detail linkage) - 3 open now: B-001, B-004, B-010
tools/project_control.py status + tools/current_state.py --json (reference algorithms to reproduce read-only; the dashboard reads files, it never invokes write subcommands)
GitHub REST (public repo martin10101/nyc-buildability, unauthenticated): GET /commits/main, /pulls?state=open, /pulls?state=closed (merged filter), /actions/runs?branch=main + /runs/{id}/jobs
docs/PRODUCT_FLOW_AND_AI_BOUNDARIES.md + PRD.md (real product journey + systems, for product-map.json), docs/GATES_AND_CHECKPOINTS.md (gate meanings)
apps/web existing tokens/classes in src/app/globals.css + src/components/property/InternalBanner.tsx + rule-evaluation feature-flag idiom (patterns to reuse, read-only)
outputs: project-control/product-map.json (NEW canonical metadata: systems, eng/launch weights summing to 100 each, owner titles/purposes, task/milestone membership, journey, readiness caps)
project-control/product-map.schema.json (NEW JSON schema for the metadata)

tools/validate_product_map.py (stdlib validator: schema + cross-reference integrity: every ledger task maps to exactly one system, weights sum to 100, referenced ids exist, journey references valid systems)
tools/test_product_map.py (stdlib unit tests for the validator + product-map integrity, runnable in the control-plane CI job)
.github/workflows/ci.yml (ADDITIVE product-map validation job only; existing jobs untouched)

apps/web/src/lib/dashboard/** (deterministic read-only engine: ledger loader, status/gate mapping, progress + launch-readiness calculators, dependency graph, activity, blockers, GitHub client, types) + co-located vitest tests

apps/web/src/app/dashboard/** (route: Mission Control + Product Map + Current Work + Roadmap + Activity + component detail; light theme scoped stylesheet; INTERNAL banner; non-public runtime flag gate)
apps/web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts (Playwright walkthrough of the owner journey + a11y)

docs/DASHBOARD.md (architecture, source-of-truth map, progress + launch-readiness formulas, GitHub integration, local run, Render notes, env vars, product-map schema, stale-data behavior, how to add systems/tasks)
requirement_refs: Owner directive 2026-07-22 (this dashboard): read-only observability over project-control; commit count rejected as progress; two distinct progress numbers (engineering vs launch readiness); owner-first plain-English; must go through the same control-plane standards (protected main, PR, CI, independent gates, acceptance); do not weaken/duplicate the control plane.

CLAUDE.md permanent principles #6 (state machine controls workflow; dashboard observes, never declares compliance), #9 (orchestrator-only control mutations - dashboard is read-only), #14 (thin-client: no new local deps/DB/Docker), #26 owner-spec (dashboard itself goes through the control plane).
docs/PRODUCT_FLOW_AND_AI_BOUNDARIES.md + PRD.md (canonical product journey + systems feeding product-map.json).
docs/GATES_AND_CHECKPOINTS.md (gate semantics surfaced to the owner).
docs/LOW_STORAGE_CLOUD_DEVELOPMENT_POLICY.md (web build/tests in CI, not on owner PC).
forbidden_paths: any rule JSON / legal rule content
any other task's project-control/tasks/*.json or gates/*.json
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 (product-map contract, G1): product-map.json validates against product-map.schema.json; every ledger task id maps to exactly one system; system eng_weights sum to 100 AND launch_weights sum to 100; every referenced task_id/milestone_id exists in the ledger; every journey step references a defined system. tools/validate_product_map.py --check exits 0 on the real file and non-zero on each deliberately broken fixture (bad weight sum, orphan task, dangling reference).

AS-2 (deterministic ledger parse, G3/G4): the engine parses a frozen project-control fixture into a typed model that reproduces project_control.py status task_counts and current_state.py per-milestone accepted/total rollups exactly (byte-for-byte on the counts).

AS-3 (owner status vocabulary, G3/G4): each config task_status (backlog, ready, claimed, in_progress, self_check, awaiting_gate, rework, accepted, blocked, canceled) maps deterministically to the owner vocabulary (PLANNED/READY/ACTIVE/TESTING/REVIEW/BLOCKED/ACCEPTED); an unknown/unexpected status maps to a safe UNKNOWN badge and never throws.

AS-4 (gate + acceptance-eligibility mapping, G3): per-task gate progress = required_gates INTERSECT gates with result PASS; acceptance-eligibility reproduces accept() (all required gates PASS with independent role + reviewer!=producer, all deps accepted, no open blocker referencing the task). Verified against M4-T005 (awaiting_gate, not acceptable) and an accepted-task fixture.

AS-5 (engineering completion, G3/G4): Engineering % = sum over systems of eng_weight * (sum task.progress_percent in system) / (100 * expected_count), expected_count = max(contracted, planned). Deterministic; matches a hand-computed expected value on the fixture; stable across runs; recomputable from source.

AS-6 (launch readiness, G3/G4): Launch % = sum over systems of launch_weight * readiness_fraction, readiness from ACCEPTED tasks only plus hard caps (rules capped until G6 legal sign-off; auth = 0 while blocked). Deterministic; differs from Engineering %; matches a hand-computed expected value on the fixture.

AS-7 (dependency graph + current work, G3): task->task and milestone depends_on graphs build from the ledger; Current Work is derived from AUTHORITATIVE lifecycle state (claimed/in_progress/self_check/awaiting_gate/rework), NOT from latest-commit heuristics; ALL legitimately-active tasks are shown and the critical-path/current-primary is identified deterministically where possible; next-task derivation matches the fixture; no cycle crashes.

AS-8 (blocker detection, G3/G4): only status:open blockers surface; linkage via affects/detail word-boundary match; the 3 real open blockers (B-001, B-004, B-010) appear; a resolved blocker does not; nothing is labeled a blocker without control-plane support.

AS-9 (biggest things preventing launch, G3/G4): derived from critical_for_beta systems below a readiness threshold + open blockers + not-started critical milestones; deterministic ordering; contains no invented items.

AS-10 (activity feed, G3): what-changed prioritizes meaningful CONTROL-PLANE events (task accepted, task started, gate PASS/FAIL, PR merged, blocker created/resolved, checkpoint, milestone/system progress changed) from ledger timestamps; raw git commits are secondary evidence, never the activity model; excludes noisy intermediate progress_log; deterministic for a fixed 'today' on the fixture.

AS-11 (GitHub parse + freshness + stale-safety, G3/G5): given recorded GitHub API JSON fixtures the client derives main SHA, open/merged PR counts, and an overall CI conclusion; the UI shows a last-synced time; on fetch failure it retains last-known data and marks it STALE, and never presents stale data as live.

AS-12 (fail-safe on missing/corrupt data, G3/G4): a missing or malformed project-control file or product-map.json yields 'Progress unavailable - project state could not be verified' rather than a fabricated number, and the app does not crash.

AS-13 (read-only + internal + security, G5): the dashboard performs no writes and no GitHub mutations; server code contains no secret/token; the route is gated by a non-public runtime flag (disabled => not served) and shows the INTERNAL banner; untrusted ledger/GitHub text renders safely (no XSS/injection); GitHub access is least-privilege read of a public repo (no SSRF).

AS-14 (owner human-journey + a11y + mobile + honesty, G4): the running dashboard answers the owner's 13 acceptance questions; is keyboard-operable; conveys status with label+symbol (never color alone); announces async GitHub load via a live region; is readable on desktop and mobile; and shows M4 rules honestly as DRAFT/needs-review, never as done/verified/published.

AS-15 (regression + zero-new-deps, G4): full apps/web lint + typecheck + build + vitest + Playwright pass in CI; existing property/confirm screens and their tests are unchanged; the additive product-map validation CI job is green; apps/web/package.json and package-lock.json are byte-identical to main (no new dependency).

AS-16 (calculation transparency + precision, G3/G4; owner req 1,2): every displayed percentage is reproducible from repository state alone and exposes a breakdown (contributing systems, their tasks, weights, denominators, any applied cap) sufficient to answer 'How is this calculated?'; the main UI shows WHOLE percentages only; extra precision appears only in detail/debug views; a number never appears more precise than the model supports.

AS-17 (health != completion; GitHub never corrupts canonical state, G3/G4/G5; owner req 3,9): system and overall HEALTH are computed independently of completion; failed CI, stale GitHub, a blocked dependency, or a newly discovered control-plane inconsistency degrade HEALTH without rewriting historical completion; project-state numbers are computed from project-control files ONLY (authoritative); GitHub is supplemental live operational state (CI/PR/head/freshness) and its unavailability never alters completion or launch-readiness.

AS-18 (UNKNOWN/DEGRADED + historical honesty, G3/G4; owner req 4,8): unknown/corrupt/missing/contradictory control-plane data is surfaced as UNKNOWN/DEGRADED and NEVER silently coerced to 0%, complete, or healthy; a task later reopened/superseded/found deficient is shown at its canonical CURRENT status, not a permanent past acceptance; a contradiction between state.json rosters and a task file's own status is flagged as a control-plane inconsistency (degrades health) rather than silently trusted.

gate_reviewer_map.G1: data-contract-verifier (product-map.json schema + validator + cross-reference integrity + weight sums; fidelity of derived-state mapping vs ledger)
gate_reviewer_map.G3: code-reviewer (engine correctness, progress + launch-readiness math, status/gate mapping, fail-safe parsing, read-only discipline, no duplicate source of truth, GitHub client)
gate_reviewer_map.G4: qa-engineer (unit/integration/regression: full web suites, fixture determinism, missing/corrupt/stale handling, zero-new-deps) AND human-journey-reviewer (owner walkthrough of all views + a11y + mobile + honesty) - BOTH must PASS
gate_reviewer_map.G5: security-reviewer (read-only proof, no secrets, non-public flag gate, no write/mutation paths, untrusted-text sanitization/XSS, least-privilege public GitHub reads, no SSRF)

producer_plan: Sequential in ONE worktree (.claude/worktrees/M0-T022-dashboard) on branch task/M0-T022-owner-dashboard off origin/main, to respect the tight product-map -> engine -> UI contract coupling; no parallel writers over overlapping files. Phase 1 (contract): author product-map.json + product-map.schema.json + stdlib validator + tests + additive CI job; validate locally with python. Phase 2 (engine): deterministic TS read-only engine (loader, status/gate mapping, progress + launch-readiness, dependency graph, activity, blockers, GitHub client) + vitest. Phase 3 (UI): /dashboard views + light-theme scoped styling + INTERNAL banner + non-public flag + a11y + e2e. Web build/lint/typecheck/vitest/Playwright validate in CI only (no local npm per thin-client policy); python validator + tests run locally and in the control-plane CI job. Orchestrator commits phases and freezes one SHA before gates.
execution_location: Cloud/worktree isolation on branch task/M0-T022-owner-dashboard; CI (GitHub Actions) is the authority for web build/lint/typecheck/vitest/e2e and the product-map validation job. Owner PC stays thin-client: text-only edits, python stdlib validation + tests locally; NO local npm, DB, Docker, or bulk data. Temporary files under the session scratchpad only.
disk_budget: Negligible (source + small JSON metadata/fixtures). No datasets, caches, or node_modules added locally; zero new npm dependencies.
holds_honored: Read-only observer: no control-plane mutation, no task/gate/acceptance change, no master_plan change, no merge to protected main outside the PR+CI+gate flow. Expansion/GDS holds untouched (this is owner-directed M0 tooling, not expansion-pack work; no 3D/UI expansion tasks contracted). Nothing Published/Verified; the dashboard reports M4 rules as draft/needs-review honestly. Existing product screens and contracts unchanged.
risks: Two progress numbers must be deterministic and auditable; any AI-guessed value or hidden heuristic fails the owner's core requirement. Mitigation: explicit weights in product-map.json + pure calculators + unit tests with hand-computed expected values.

Product-map.json is the only NEW source of truth; if it drifts from the ledger (orphan tasks, stale references) the dashboard misleads. Mitigation: validator enforces every task maps to exactly one system + all references resolve, run in CI.

Must remain strictly read-only and internal; a write path, secret, or public exposure would break the observer contract. Mitigation: no write/CLI calls, no token (public repo), non-public runtime flag + INTERNAL banner, G5 security gate.

Deployed Render slug may not contain project-control/ at runtime. Mitigation for V1: robust repo-root discovery + fail-safe 'unavailable'; document the build-time snapshot option for Render; V1 target is local-first + Render-ready.

Zero-new-dependency constraint (thin client) rules out React Flow/ajv; the graph and schema validation are hand-built. Mitigation: built-in SVG/CSS map + stdlib python validator + defensive TS parsing.

progress_log.message: Phase 1 start: authoring product-map.json + schema + stdlib validator; then TS engine, then /dashboard UI. Contract-first, single worktree.

Phase 1 (data contract) built + committed c7404c7: product-map.json (55 tasks -> 10 systems, weights sum 100), schema, stdlib validator, 13 unit tests (all green), additive product-map CI job. Next: Phase 2 TS read-only engine (progress + launch-readiness calculators) + vitest.
